etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "torment" + "-ed" from Latin "tormentum" + "-ed" (having been twisted, tortured)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: Having been twisted tortured; experiencing afflicted distressed and tormented affliction supported by healing awareness
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Having been twisted tortured → afflicted/distressed → contemporary tormented and being tormented afflicted and tormented distressed with developmental development
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: Having been twisted tortured → afflicted/distressed → tormented → understanding development

contemporary_usage.primary_definition: Definition**: Being tormented afflicted and tormented distressed; experiencing afflicted distressed and tormented tormentation supported by healing awareness restoration
contemporary_usage.register: Severe negative; indicates affliction calling for immediate healing support and compassionate restoration

- **Colloquial Usage**: "Tormented soul," "tormented by guilt," "tormented conscience"
